title: viennaRectifier
description: The description of a Vienna rectifier (3-phase 3-level boost-type PFC, unidirectional). Three identical line-frequency boost inductors carry pure-AC line current. Switch blocking voltage = Vdc/2 (3-level advantage). Originally Kolar & Zach 1994; modern SiC industrial implementations: TI TIDA-010257, ST STDES-VIENNARECT, Microchip MSCSICPFC-REF5. See src/converter_models/VIENNA_PLAN.md for the full design rationale.

lineToLineVoltage.description: AC line-to-line RMS voltage (e.g. 400 V for EU, 480 V for US)

outputDcVoltage.description: Total DC bus voltage Vdc = Vdc+ + |Vdc-| (typical 700-800 V)

viennaVariant.description: Vienna switch arrangement: viennaI = single bidirectional switch per leg (3 switches total, classic Kolar 1994); viennaII = two switches per leg (reduced conduction loss at >30 kW)

switchType.description: How each per-leg bidirectional switch is realised: tType (modern SiC default, two anti-series MOSFETs to neutral); backToBackMosfet; singleMosfetIn4DiodeBridge (Kolar 1994 classic)

synchronousRectifier.description: If true, the 6 fast rectifier diodes are replaced with MOSFETs

phaseCount.description: Number of interleaved Vienna channels in parallel (1 = single Vienna, >1 = multi-channel for >50 kW)

samplingStrategy.description: How the line-cycle envelope is sampled: peakOfLineOnly = single sample at omega*t = pi/2 (cheapest); peakOfLinePlusSectors = peak + 30/60-degree sector samples; fullLineCycle = 36 samples per cycle (THD validation only)

viennaRectifierOperatingPoint.description: The description of one Vienna rectifier operating point
viennaRectifierOperatingPoint.title: viennaRectifierOperatingPoint
